mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month when policy is moving down rather than flat or up.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month falling FEDFUNDS trend, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS indicates improving payroll growth and income momentum, which supports consumption and earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than untriggered observations, or if the signal flips negative after accounting for payroll revision windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in RSAFS above its recent trend signals stronger retail demand and near-term revenue growth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend surprises are followed by negative or zero 5d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with steady or falling CPIAUCSL signals real output growth without immediate inflation pressure, which supports margins and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal of higher INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL does not outperform either series alone for 20d returns, or if triggered trades have negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals labor deterioration and higher recession risk, so equity multiples contract and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold events are not followed by below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal fails to hold in recession-like periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains unusually low, real rates stay suppressed and the market prices eventual policy support, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up/low-rate episodes do not lead to positive 60d SPX returns, or if the effect is eliminated once FEDFUNDS rises above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior rebound signals cooling housing demand and weaker cyclically sensitive spending, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average after a 2-month rise
failure_condition: Reject if post-reversal HOUST declines are not associated with negative 20d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in more than half of rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, stronger INDPRO only helps equities if it is not accompanied by rising UNRATE; otherwise output gains are illusory. Positive 60-day SPX returns require industrial production strength with stable labor conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if high-INDPRO/low-UNRATE periods do not outperform other periods for 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is no better than random after controlling for revisions.
